text:
Market today: Tech is mixed. AI fears hit some names like Adobe, but Nvidia's investment in cloud/AI shows underlying strength. Geopolitical tensions and China's potential AI export controls add a layer of uncertainty, especially for your chip names. Jamie Dimon's caution is a broad market warning.

Your holdings: SOXL is down a massive -46.24% and needs immediate attention due to its leveraged nature and decay. Your new SPAL position is also leveraged and already down -51.33%. QQQ and SMH are also showing "SELL" signals, with SMH down -12.19%. Remember, margin amplifies these drops significantly.

Watch: Keep a very close eye on any news regarding China's AI export controls – this could directly hit your core holdings. Also, monitor your leveraged ETFs closely; their stops are critical.

Bottom line: Manage your significant leveraged losses and stay defensive on your core tech/chip exposure given the mixed signals and geopolitical risks.
plays: SOXL is down -46.24%. Respect the stop at 200.00; this leveraged ETF's decay combined with current losses makes it a high-risk hold.
SPAL, a new leveraged position, is down -51.33%. Consider trimming or respecting the stop at 21.38 to manage this volatile exposure.
No new buys today - hold and watch. Focus on managing existing positions given the market's mixed signals and your current drawdowns.
